title: Epic: Secure Obfuscated Server Configuration Viewer
body:
### Description
Implement an obfuscated, read-only server configuration viewer in the administrator dashboard, governed by role-access settings in the server configuration file. Any sensitive credentials or webhook URLs must be masked in the payload before being returned by the API.

### Requirements
- **Configuration Role Protection Option**: Add `view_config_allowed_role` setting (accepting `owner` or `admin`, defaulting to `owner`) controlling who can access the configuration viewer.
- **Sensitive Fields Masking**: Obfuscate fields such as `smtp_server.password`, `webhooks.slack_url`, `webhooks.teams_url`, `sso_providers[i].client_secret`, `edge_token`, and `edge_nodes[i].token_hash`.
- **Read-Only Tree View UI**: Design a gorgeous interactive JSON/YAML tree browser in the settings/configuration tab of the Admin Dashboard.

issues.title: Sub-Issue #1: Implement Server-Side Config View API with Masking and Access Options
issues.body:
### Tasks
1. Add `ViewConfigAllowedRole` (`view_config_allowed_role`) to `ServerConfig` struct in `pkg/config/config.go` (defaulting to `owner`).
2. Register `GET /api/admin/config-view` route inside `pkg/server/server.go`.
3. Validate requester authorization role against the configured `ViewConfigAllowedRole` (returning `403 Forbidden` if unauthorized).
4. Deep-copy the running configuration and mask all sensitive values with `[MASKED]` string values.
5. Write unit tests verifying masking compliance and role authorization checks.

issues.title: Sub-Issue #2: Add Masked Config Tree View Panel in Admin Dashboard UI
issues.body:
### Tasks
1. Create a styled collapsable config viewer container in the Admin Dashboard Settings view.
2. Fetch configuration JSON from `/api/admin/config-view` dynamically (verifying role authorization capability beforehand).
3. Render the JSON structure using a beautiful interactive nested tree node format (supporting collapse/expand tree toggles).
4. Integrate dynamic search/filter capability across key-value configuration tree nodes.
